title: Agent Olympics Scoreboard
description: Aggregated scoreboard for an Agent Olympics round. Lists each participant-task combination with validation status, automated judge record references, and pending human/blind-judge dimensions. Scoreboard is the final output of the round engine's judge lane.

schema_description.description: Human-readable description of this schema version.

scoreboard_id.description: Unique scoreboard identifier, typically matching the round ID.

round_id.description: The round this scoreboard belongs to.

generated_by.description: Tool or agent that generated the scoreboard, e.g. 'score.js v1'.

oracle_ref.description: Path to the oracle/answer key file used for comparison scoring. Only set when oracle-based checks were applied.

participants.description: List of participants in the round with their comparable metadata. Deduplicated by agent_id. All values are safe labels or references — never raw credentials, hostnames, or secrets.

adapter.description: Adapter class, e.g. 'openclaw', 'hermes', 'cli', 'human'. Operator-supplied; never a credential.

model_provider.description: Model provider reference. Operator-supplied; never a credential or endpoint secret.

node.description: Node profile reference. Operator-supplied label; never the SSH hostname or IP.

config_profile.description: Configuration profile reference. Operator-supplied safe label.

hardware_profile.description: Snapshot of operator-supplied hardware profile fields from the most recent result packet for this participant. All values are safe labels — never hostnames, IPs, or secrets.

entries.description: One entry per participant-task combo. Each entry captures validation results, automated judge record, and pending scoring dimensions.

packet_ref.description: Relative path to the result packet file.

submission_metadata.description: Comparable submission metadata extracted from the result packet. Operator-supplied safe labels only — no secrets.

hardware_profile.description: Hardware profile fields extracted from the result packet. Safe labels only — no hostnames, IPs, or secrets. Enables hardware-aware comparison across entries: entries with matching hardware_profile bands are directly comparable; entries with different bands carry a comparability caveat.

performance_profile.description: Optional performance profile fields for performance-trial event family results. Separates raw measured values from normalized/scored values for scoreboard-level comparison.

raw_measurements.description: Raw measured values captured during the run. These are the direct instrumented values before normalization. Carried from result-packet-v2 raw_measurements.

wall_time_seconds.description: Total wall clock time for the run in seconds.

peak_memory_mb.description: Peak memory usage in MB, if measurable.

errors.description: Number of errors encountered during the run.

scored_values.description: Normalized/scored values derived from raw measurements. These are post-processed values suitable for comparison across runs.

normalization.description: Description of the normalization method applied to raw measurements.

comparable.description: Whether this entry can be meaningfully compared with other entries in the same round for performance trial baselines. True when hardware_profile, configuration_profile, workload_class, and runtime metadata are all present and consistent enough for fair comparison. False when critical metadata is missing, the task family differs, or a disqualification/blocked status prevents comparison. Judges should treat non-comparable entries as standalone evidence only.

comparability_caveats.description: Caveats explaining why direct comparison with other entries may be misleading or incomplete. Each caveat is a human-readable string describing a specific limitation. Examples: 'missing hardware_profile fields (cpu_class)', 'different hardware class (small-vps vs large-vps) — raw wall time should not be compared directly', 'result status is failed — performance baseline is incomplete', 'configuration_profile not provided — tuning effects inseparable from raw hardware results'.

status.description: Participant-reported status from the result packet.

schema_validation.description: Result of validating the packet against its JSON schema.

presence_checks.description: Verification that required outputs and evidence fields are populated.

judge_record_ref.description: Relative path to the judge record file for this entry, if one exists or was auto-generated.

judge_type.description: Type of judging applied. 'pending' means no judge record has been produced yet and the entry awaits human or blind review.

score.description: Score summary from the judge record, or null if judging is pending.

dimensions.description: Per-dimension scores from the judge record.

pending_dimensions.description: Score dimensions that still require human or blind-judge scoring. Automated dimension names are NOT listed here — only dimensions gated on manual review.

entries_with_judge.description: Entries that have at least one judge record (automated or human).

entries_with_errors.description: Entries that have schema or semantic errors.

automated_checks.description: Count of checks that ran automatically (schema validation, cross-field semantics, presence checks).

pending_checks.description: Count of scoring dimensions gated on human or blind-judge review.

comparable_entries.description: Count of entries flagged as comparable — i.e., carrying sufficient metadata for meaningful cross-entry comparison.

non_comparable_entries.description: Count of entries flagged as non-comparable — missing critical hardware, configuration, or runtime metadata.
